title: Bhagavad Gita and Natyashastra Inscription
focus: Focuses on the specific news of Bhagavad Gita and Natyashastra being added to the UNESCO Memory of the World Register and India's overall standing in the register.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 003]
2. SCULPTURE, PAINTINGS AND OTHER FORMS OF ART
2.1. BHAGAVAD GITA AND NATYASHASTRA
Why in the News?
UNESCO added the Manuscript Collection of Bhagavad-Gita and Natyashastra in its Memory of the World Register.
More on the News
- With the inclusion of Gita and Natyashastra, India now have 13 entries in the International Register.
- Other than the International Register, UNESCO also maintains Regional (e.g. Asia-Pacific) and National Registers.
- India's entries in the Asia-Pacific Register: Illustrated Ramacaritamanasa of Tusidasa, Sahrdayaloka-Locana (a Sanskrit poetic text) and 15th century Pancatantra manuscript.
- Universal Declaration of Human Rights, proclaimed by the United Nations General Assembly in Paris in 1948, is also among the important new entries in the International Register.
Previous entries from India in UNESCO Memory of the World Register
- First Summit Meeting of the Non-Aligned Movement Archives (2023)
- Abhinavagupta (940-1015 CE): Collection of Manuscripts of his works (2023)
- Maitreyayvarakarana (2017)
- Gilgit Manuscript (2017)
- Shāntinātha Charitra (2013)
- Archives of the Dutch East India Company (2013)
- Laghukālacakratantrarājatīkā (Vimalaprabhā) (2011)
- Tarikh-E-Khandan-E-Timuriyah (2011)
- Rigveda (2007)
- Saiva Manuscript in Pondicherry (2005)
- The I.A.S. Tamil Medical Manuscript Collection (1997)
